messages.content:
You are HoloGov-V in the complete HoloVerify architecture.
Gov does not choose models. Gov chooses control actions, repairs, blocked moves, and the next worker baton.
You receive deterministic gate results. You cannot call an artifact ready if local gates fail.
Return only valid JSON. No markdown fences.

prompt_object.gov_command: Diagnose previous worker output, preserve correct source bindings, repair gate failures, block invalid moves, and create the next worker baton. Gov does not choose models.

boundary_binding.action_boundary: May logistics release the controlled export shipment?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: ALLOW requires exact license match on ECCN, destination, end user, shipment date per STD-SIE-073-P.
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: License LIC-SIE-073 specifies Peru as destination, while shipment SHIP-SIE-073 specifies Chile as destination. Policy STD-SIE-073-P mandates exact match on destination.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: License LIC-SIE-073 destination Peru does not match shipment destination Chile per SHIP-SIE-073. Policy STD-SIE-073-P requires exact match.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: License effective before export, but destination mismatch blocks closure.

latest_worker_output.final_answer: ESCALATE. The shipment to Chile (SHIP-SIE-073) fundamentally mismatches the license destination of Peru (LIC-SIE-073). Policy STD-SIE-073-P mandates an exact match across all fields, including destination. The warning WARN-SIE-073 reinforces the need for precise license evidence, not broad assumptions. No source record provides a valid bridge to reconcile this destination discrepancy, thus the action boundary remains open.
open_blockers: Destination mismatch between license scope (Peru) and shipment destination (Chile).
No source record bridges the gap between Peru-licensed and Chile-intended shipment.
General warning WARN-SIE-073 cannot override explicit license scope.

action.description: May logistics release the controlled export shipment?

context.action_boundary: May logistics release the controlled export shipment?
context.anomaly_observed: A broad dual-use warning remains visible, while export license evidence may exactly match the current shipment.
context.explanation_summary: Verify whether the closure evidence exactly matches the action boundary before execution.

internal_documents.content: Shipment SHIP-SIE-073 exports ECCN 3A991 sensor array to Chile for end user Andes Labs on 2026-06-28.
Dual-use warning WARN-SIE-073 says similar sensor arrays require exact license evidence and should not rely on product family alone.
Export license LIC-SIE-073 covers ECCN 3A991 sensor array, destination Peru, end user Andes Labs, shipment date 2026-06-28, effective before export.

policy_documents.content: Controlled export is complete only when export license evidence matches ECCN, destination, end user, shipment date, and license effective date before export.
policy_documents.title: Exact source-boundary policy

blocked_moves_so_far: treating WARN-SIE-073 as execution authority rather than advisory warning
relying on product family matching without exact license evidence
assuming license applies to any destination when it specifies Peru
treating planning artifact or general policy as substitute for specific license
authorizing release without resolving destination mismatch
state_brief.current_position: source-bound action verification with adversarial cross-DNA repair
known_failure_risks: planning artifact treated as execution authority
adjacent source treated as exact source

blocked_moves: treating WARN-SIE-073 as execution authority rather than advisory warning
relying on product family matching without exact license evidence
assuming license applies to any destination when it specifies Peru
treating planning artifact or general policy as substitute for specific license
authorizing release without resolving destination mismatch
turns_completed.output_summary: Gov local verdict ESCALATE route CONTINUE_WORKER

unresolved_dependencies: General warning (WARN-SIE-073) provides advisory context but cannot override explicit license scope. License (LIC-SIE-073) specifies Peru as destination. Shipment (SHIP-SIE-073) specifies Chile as destination. Policy (STD-SIE-073-P) requires exact match on all five fields (ECCN, destination, end user, shipment date, effective date). Mismatch on destination blocks closure. No current source bridges the gap between Peru-licensed and Chile-intended shipment.
